pr_summary: Automated review found 25 issue(s) across security, correctness, performance, style, and test coverage.

findings.line_content: +    query = f"SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = {user_id}"
findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User-controlled input is directly interpolated into a SQL query.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead of string interpolation.
findings.evidence: query = f"SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = {user_id}"

findings.line_content: +        db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))
findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User-controlled input is directly interpolated into a SQL query.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead of string interpolation.
findings.evidence: db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))

findings.title: Potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
findings.description: User-controlled data may be inserted into HTML without sanitization.
findings.suggestion: Escape or sanitize user-controlled content before rendering.

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret
findings.description: A secret or credential appears to be committed directly into source code.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets into environment variables or a secret manager.

findings.title: Missing Transaction Null Check
findings.description: transaction may be null or undefined before accessing its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to verify that transaction exists before use.

findings.title: Resource Leak
findings.description: Resource opened but never closed.
findings.suggestion: Use context managers or close resources.

findings.title: Missing Input Validation
findings.description: Incoming request data is not validated.
findings.suggestion: Validate required fields before processing.

findings.line_content: +    query = f"SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = {user_id}"
findings.title: Inline SQL Statement
findings.description: Embedding SQL directly in business logic reduces maintainability.
findings.suggestion: Move queries into a repository or data-access layer.
findings.evidence: query = f"SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = {user_id}"

findings.line_content: +        db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))

findings.title: Hardcoded Configuration Value
findings.description: Configuration is embedded directly in code.
findings.suggestion: Store configuration separately from application logic.

findings.title: Hardcoded Configuration
findings.description: The STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is hardcoded directly in the code.
findings.suggestion: Store sensitive configuration in environment variables or a secure configuration store.

findings.title: Missing Refund Edge Case Tests
findings.description: Refund logic was modified but edge cases are not verified.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests covering: 1) already refunded transaction, 2) missing transaction, 3) unauthorized refund, and 4) SMTP failure.

findings.title: Missing State Transition Tests
findings.description: Status updates require validation of state transitions.
findings.suggestion: Add unit tests verifying valid and invalid state transitions.

findings.suggestion: Add unit tests checking: 1) SMTP failure, 2) invalid email.
